title: Why did everyone want third impact anyway
display_title: Why did everyone want third impact anyway
wikitext:
[]: Why did everyone want third impact anyway?             Quote Post by xfactor30seconds » Tue Mar 17, 2009 3:55 pm I'm sorry I just have no comprehension (and no spelling skills so bear with me) why anyone would want third impact to occur Everyone dies including Gendo and SEELE so wtf you know.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Tue Mar 17, 2009 4:02 pm xfactor30seconds wrote: no spelling skills so bear with me

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Tue Mar 17, 2009 5:47 pm My mistake. I got the words bear and bare momentarily mixed up. Me know much English good, yes?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Mar 17, 2009 5:47 pm I'm not sure that "want" is necessarily part of it -- the discussion between Yui and Kozo at the reservoir in episode 21' gives the impression that 3I -- or at least an event of such a magnitude as to merit the name -- is an inevitable thing, and the difference is more in who seizes control and how they guide it.

[]:             Quote Post by xfactor30seconds » Tue Mar 17, 2009 6:26 pm i wasnt sure it was an inevitability so much as Gendo and SEELE wanted to finish the instrumentality project. The whole project was to create a colony of flawed and separate individuals and basically combine them. To have artificial evolution in mankind and basically make a super human. So I just always thought it was just so mankind could evolve and mature or die out so a new race could dominate until it became corrupted as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Twsa'teotu » Tue Mar 17, 2009 6:29 pm Seele wanted to be gods

[]:             Quote Post by LIGHTFIRELT » Tue Mar 17, 2009 7:14 pm Not everyone per say, especially not the common people. A couple people just had different perceptions of how humanity should exist.

[]:             Quote Post by rberlin » Tue Mar 17, 2009 8:31 pm Seele and Keel seemed happy with the way Instrumentality went down, despite not appearing to get anything extra out of the situation. And with what Ritsuko said about humans being at the end of our evolution, the Chamber of Gauf(?) being empty and killing Ireul by accelerating her evolution, that Instrumentality project was the only hope for humanity. Gendo had selfish reasons for wanting Instrumentality to go a certain way, but he probably couldn't have prevented it even if he had opposed it. In the end he just wanted to see his wife again. One thing I'm hoping Rebuild will clear up is why he wanted into Seele in the first place. And since only the two most messed up people from the series ended up rejecting Instrumentality, probably speaks to Seele's benevolence.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Tue Mar 17, 2009 8:41 pm Hopefully in Rebuild we'll see a whole new scenario play out.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Mar 17, 2009 10:04 pm rberlin wrote: humans being at the end of our evolution

[]:             Quote Post by rberlin » Tue Mar 17, 2009 10:15 pm I don't see how that is possible as interconnected as world populations are nowadays. It's not like the Sahara Desert, Caucasus Mountains or Pacific Ocean are barriers to separate us anymore. And as far as evolution speeding up, since evolution can only go one way I agree. But it will seem like devolution since we're eliminating all the evolutionary forces that would cause us to adapt to our environment and simply reshaping the environment to suit our ever increasing frailties. Either way, I was specifically talking about the world of Eva, where humans were at the end of evolution and were going to start dieing off if we didn't jump start evolution.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Tue Mar 17, 2009 10:19 pm physical barriers are gone but you still dont have much racial mixing, even tho its viewed as ok by most people. Its human nature to stick close to the people closest like you, it evolved when we sere still warring tribes.

[]:             Quote Post by pablumatic » Tue Mar 17, 2009 10:33 pm Third Impact was essentially inevitable in the Evangelion universe since it was so much like our own only with anime body proportions, physics, and tangible spirituality. Somebody, somewhere would have found Adam and brought about Second Impact which would inevitably lead to the third by either the same people who started the second or the awakened angels. Its just human nature to screw with total annihilation. Mankind's obsession with building world destroying nuclear arsenals is proof of that. And more often than not in our own reality the craziest and most violent among us make it to the top of the heap and guide civilization for their own machinations. I'm certain that if we lived in a universe where apocalypse causing deities were buried in a couple spots on the globe, they'd ultimately be unearthed and unleashed to their full apocalyptic nature because that's just how we are (well at least a large portion who are in charge anyway).

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Mar 17, 2009 10:40 pm The chief environmental factors driving human evolution now are changing constantly due to technology and culture, and these are not all going in the same direction or at the same pace. The barriers that separate people now include politics, religion, status, and economics as well as geography.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Mar 19, 2009 8:37 am This is all getting a bit off-topic, but I thought I'd add: We all remained one species during the early development of Homo sapiens over many, many thousands of years, while the various races differentiated. The forces that keep human populations "isolated" from one another in the modern globalized era are really quite transient and fickle in the grand scheme of things. It's incredibly unlikely that any kind of segregation could be sustained -- pending colonization of outer space, anyway -- to the point where any two populations will genetically differentiate to the point where they can no longer interbreed -- that is, to the point of becoming two truly distinct species*. Even an apparently low level of e.g. interracial mixing in certain xenophobic populations may be enough to thwart "efforts at speciation", maintained over the long term. People just get shuffled around way too much to make "multiple human species evolving naturally" scenarios in any way realistic. Do something about globalization -- well, then maybe. * Sometimes "species" lines are drawn at more arbitrary levels, i.e. to separate wild from domestic varieties even if they can produce fully viable offspring, but we're going to ignore those cases just to simplify the usage of "species" here.

[]:             Quote Post by master0rolando » Wed Mar 25, 2009 3:54 am i always took from it that Seele, Gendo, and Yui and all of them were obsessed with having control... they figure that the end would come, and if it was...it should happen on their terms...

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Mar 25, 2009 9:38 am I'm just passing along what I read. Check it out for yourself in the March 09 issue of Discover.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Tue Mar 17, 2009 4:02 pm xfac
